covered_indications: ANCA-Associated Vasculitis (granulomatosis with polyangiitis / Wegener's granulomatosis, microscopic polyangiitis) - induction and maintenance
Pemphigus Vulgaris - initial, relapse, and maintenance
Rheumatoid Arthritis - moderately to severely active disease
Autoimmune Hemolytic Anemia (warm AIHA, cold hemagglutinin disease, mixed AIHA)
Graft-Versus-Host Disease (chronic, steroid-refractory)
Hematopoietic Cell Transplantation (conditioning for allogeneic transplant)
Immune Thrombocytopenia (ITP)

Immunotherapy-Related Toxicities Associated with Checkpoint Inhibitors (corticosteroid-refractory dermatologic, hematologic, myasthenia gravis, immune-mediated encephalitis, myositis, stage 3 acute kidney injury)
Interstitial Lung Disease Associated with Systemic Autoimmune Rheumatic Disease (SARD-ILD)
Membranous Nephropathy (moderate or high risk for progressive kidney function loss)
Minimal Change Disease (frequently relapsing or steroid-dependent)
Multiple Sclerosis
Myasthenia Gravis (anti-MuSK antibody-positive)
Neuromyelitis Optica Spectrum Disorder
Pediatric Nephrotic Syndrome (age ≤18)
Solid Organ Transplantation (desensitization or antibody-mediated rejection)
Systemic Lupus Erythematosus (SLE), including nephrotic syndrome in SLE
Thrombotic Thrombocytopenic Purpura (TTP)
Antineutrophil Cytoplasmic Antibody (ANCA)-Associated Vasculitis (induction and follow-up therapy)
Pemphigus Vulgaris (initial, relapse, and maintenance therapy)
Rheumatoid Arthritis
Graft-Versus-Host Disease (chronic)
Hematopoietic Cell Transplantation
Immunotherapy-Related Toxicities Associated with Checkpoint Inhibitors
Neuromyelitis Optica Spectrum Disorder (anti-aquaporin-4 antibody positive)
Systemic Lupus Erythematosus (SLE)
Autoimmune Hemolytic Anemia
Interstitial Lung Disease Associated with Systemic Autoimmune Rheumatic Disease
Membranous Nephropathy
Minimal Change Disease
Pediatric Nephrotic Syndrome
Solid Organ Transplantation (desensitization therapy and antibody-mediated rejection)
Thrombotic Thrombocytopenic Purpura
quantity_limits: Indication-specific dosing limits: ANCA-Associated Vasculitis induction: 375 mg/m2 x4 doses (≥7 days apart) OR up to two 1,000 mg doses (≥2 weeks apart); ANCA maintenance (≥18 yrs): up to 1,000 mg x6 doses; (<18 yrs): up to 250 mg/m2 x2 doses. Pemphigus Vulgaris initial/relapse: up to two 1,000 mg doses (≥2 weeks apart); maintenance: up to 500 mg every 6 months. Rheumatoid Arthritis: up to two 1,000 mg doses per course (≥2 weeks apart), minimum 16 weeks between courses. Autoimmune Hemolytic Anemia: 375 mg/m2 OR 100 mg x4 doses (≥7 days apart). Graft-Versus-Host Disease: up to 375 mg/m2, doses ≥7 days apart. Hematopoietic Cell Transplantation: 375 mg/m2 x1 before transplant, then 1,000 mg/m2 x3 doses (≥7 days apart) after transplant. ITP: up to 375 mg/m2, doses ≥7 days apart; minimum 6 months between courses. Immunotherapy-Related Toxicities: up to 500 mg/m2 or 1,000 mg x2 doses (≥14 days apart) OR 375 mg/m2 x4 doses (≥7 days apart). SARD-ILD: up to two 1,000 mg doses (≥2 weeks apart) per course; minimum 24 weeks between courses. Membranous Nephropathy: 1,000 mg x2 doses (≥14 days apart) OR 375 mg/m2 up to 4 doses (≥7 days apart). Minimal Change Disease: 1,000 mg up to 2 doses (≥14 days apart) OR 375 mg/m2 up to 4 doses (≥7 days apart). Multiple Sclerosis: up to 2,000 mg total per course over 1 month; minimum 6 months between courses. Myasthenia Gravis: 1,000 mg x2 doses (≥14 days apart) OR 375 mg/m2 x4 doses (±2 additional monthly doses, up to 6 total) OR 750 mg/m2 x2 doses (≥14 days apart). NMOSD: up to 375 mg/m2 x4 doses (≥7 days apart) OR two 1,000 mg doses (≥2 weeks apart). Pediatric Nephrotic Syndrome: 375 mg/m2 up to 4 doses (≥7 days apart). Solid Organ Transplantation: per transplant center protocol. SLE: dose per prescriber request; minimum 6 months between courses. TTP: up to 375 mg/m2 x4 doses (≥7 days apart).
prior_auth_criteria: Prior Authorization required for all rituximab IV products. Approval requires: (1) patient meets indication-specific clinical criteria; (2) medication prescribed by or in consultation with a specialist in the condition being treated; (3) preferred product criteria met (for Rituxan: must have tried all three biosimilars - Truxima, Riabni, and Ruxience - and cannot continue due to formulation difference in inactive ingredients that would result in significant allergy or serious adverse reaction, with documentation required). Indication-specific criteria include: ANCA-Associated Vasculitis (induction: combination with glucocorticoids, prescribed by rheumatologist/nephrologist/pulmonologist/immunologist; maintenance: disease control achieved, at least 16 weeks between courses); Pemphigus Vulgaris (initial: combination with corticosteroid, prescribed by dermatologist; relapse/maintenance: min 16 weeks between courses); Rheumatoid Arthritis (initial: trial of one conventional synthetic DMARD for at least 3 months, not used concurrently with another biologic or targeted synthetic DMARD, prescribed by rheumatologist; subsequent courses: 16 weeks between courses, evidence of clinical response for 3+ courses); Autoimmune Hemolytic Anemia (prescribed by hematologist); Graft-Versus-Host Disease (chronic GVHD, trial of at least one systemic medication, prescribed by oncologist/hematologist/transplant physician); Hematopoietic Cell Transplantation (conditioning regimen for allogeneic transplant, prescribed by oncologist/hematologist/transplant physician); ITP (trial of one other therapy, prescribed by hematologist; repeat courses: 6 months between courses, prior response and relapse documented); Immunotherapy-Related Toxicities (toxicity developed on checkpoint inhibitor, symptomatic despite systemic corticosteroid trial, prescribed by specialist); Interstitial Lung Disease/SARD-ILD (age ≥18, confirmed by high-resolution CT, prescribed by pulmonologist or rheumatologist; repeat: 24 weeks between courses, beneficial response documented); Membranous Nephropathy (moderate or high risk for progressive kidney function loss, prescribed by nephrologist); Minimal Change Disease (frequently relapsing or steroid-dependent, prescribed by nephrologist); Multiple Sclerosis (inadequate efficacy or significant intolerance to at least two other disease-modifying agents, not used concurrently with another DMT, prescribed by neurologist/MS specialist, at least 6 months between courses; for 1+ year of therapy: evidence of clinical response required); Myasthenia Gravis (anti-MuSK antibody-positive, prior/current pyridostigmine or intolerance, trial of at least one immunosuppressant, unresolved symptoms, prescribed by neurologist); Neuromyelitis Optica Spectrum Disorder (prescribed by neurologist); Pediatric Nephrotic Syndrome (age ≤18, trial of systemic corticosteroid or glucocorticoid-sparing agent, prescribed by nephrologist); Solid Organ Transplantation (desensitization or antibody-mediated rejection, prescribed by transplant physician); SLE (trial of at least one standard immunomodulating/immunosuppressant agent, prescribed by rheumatologist/nephrologist/neurologist; repeat: 6 months between courses); Thrombotic Thrombocytopenic Purpura (combination with systemic corticosteroids and therapeutic plasma exchange, prescribed by hematologist).
site_of_care: Intravenous infusion (setting not explicitly specified, but intravenous administration required)
notes: This policy covers non-oncology indications only; oncology indications and Rituxan Hycela (rituximab/hyaluronidase) are addressed separately under Oncology Medications policy #1403. Biosimilars (Riabni, Ruxience, Truxima) are preferred products; Rituxan is non-preferred and requires documented trial of all three biosimilars and documented inability to continue each due to clinically significant allergy/adverse reaction related to inactive ingredient formulation differences. Biosimilars have not demonstrated interchangeability. Initial approval requires prescription by or in consultation with a specialist in the condition being treated. Requests for doses outside established dosing are considered case-by-case by Medical Director or Pharmacist. Any other non-oncology use not listed is considered not medically necessary. Documentation is required where noted in criteria. HCPCS codes: J9312 (rituximab 10 mg), Q5115 (rituximab-abbs/Truxima 10 mg), Q5119 (rituximab-pvvr/Ruxience 10 mg), Q5123 (rituximab-arrx/Riabni 10 mg). Policy number IP0319.
step_therapy_drugs: conventional synthetic DMARDs (methotrexate, leflunomide, hydroxychloroquine, sulfasalazine) for rheumatoid arthritis
pyridostigmine and at least one immunosuppressant for myasthenia gravis
standard immunomodulating or immunosuppressant agents for SLE
at least one systemic medication for graft-versus-host disease
at least one other therapy for ITP
corticosteroids/prednisone/methylprednisolone (for SLE, ITP, pediatric nephrotic syndrome, TTP)
mycophenolate (for SLE, myasthenia gravis, pediatric nephrotic syndrome)
cyclophosphamide (for SLE, myasthenia gravis, pediatric nephrotic syndrome)

systemic corticosteroids - ruxolitinib/Jakafi, belumosudil/Rezurock, axatilimab-csfr/Niktimvo, cyclosporine, tacrolimus, mycophenolate mofetil, ibrutinib/Imbruvica, imatinib, hydroxychloroquine, methotrexate, pentostatin/Nipent, interleukin-2/aldesleukin/Proleukin, sirolimus, etanercept product (for GVHD)
disease-modifying agents for multiple sclerosis (at least two required; see Appendix B)
azathioprine, cyclosporine, mycophenolate mofetil, methotrexate, tacrolimus, cyclophosphamide, nipocalimab-aahu/Imaavy, rozanolixizumab-noli/Rystiggo (for myasthenia gravis)
tacrolimus, cyclosporine, cyclophosphamide, mycophenolate mofetil (for pediatric nephrotic syndrome)
access_status: Biosimilars (Riabni, Ruxience, Truxima) are preferred; Rituxan is non-preferred and requires trial of all three biosimilars first
